3GPP_Reference.Title: NG-RAN F1 Application Protocol (F1AP), NG-RAN Overall Description; Stage 2

Feature_Description: The UE Context Setup procedure is used to establish the UE Context including, among others, SRB, DRB, BH RLC channel, Uu Relay RLC channel, PC5 Relay RLC channel, and SL DRB configuration. This procedure is specifically used for the case when the UE moves from one gNB-DU to another gNB-DU within the same gNB-CU during NR operation for L1/L2 Triggered Mobility (LTM).

The gNB-CU initiates the procedure by sending a UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message to the candidate gNB-DU. If the candidate gNB-DU succeeds in establishing the UE context and LTM configuration, it replies with a UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message. If no UE-associated logical F1-connection exists, it shall be established as part of this procedure.
Feature_Objectives: To establish the UE Context at the candidate gNB-DU for an Inter-gNB-DU LTM handover.
To configure LTM-specific resources and parameters at the candidate gNB-DU, such as CSI resource configuration, lower layer reference configuration, and LTM configuration ID mapping.
To enable early synchronization (early TA acquisition) at the candidate gNB-DU for the LTM procedure.
To facilitate UE mobility between different gNB-DUs under the same gNB-CU using LTM.

Protocol_Layers: F1-U (User Plane - implied by bearer setup)

Feature_Implementation_Steps: 1. The gNB-CU determines to initiate LTM configuration based on L3 Measurement Reports from the UE.

2. The gNB-CU sends a UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message to the candidate gNB-DU(s) for each candidate cell. This message contains the candidate cell ID, CSI resource configuration for subsequent LTM, and optionally the LTM configuration ID mapping list. The gNB-CU may also request PRACH resources or lower layer configuration from the candidate gNB-DU(s), or provide the lower layer part of the reference configuration.

3. If the candidate gNB-DU accepts the request for LTM configuration, it responds with a UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message. This response includes the generated lower layer RRC configurations for the accepted target candidate cell, and potentially early synchronization information.
Procedure_Implementation_Steps: 1. The UE sends a MeasurementReport message (L3 measurement result) to the source gNB-DU containing measurements of neighbouring cells. The source gNB-DU sends an UL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER message conveying the received MeasurementReport message to the gNB-CU (TS 38.401, Section 8.2.1.5, Step 1).
2. The gNB-CU determines to initiate LTM configuration (TS 38.401, Section 8.2.1.5, Step 2).

3. The gNB-CU sends a UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message to the candidate gNB-DU(s) for each candidate cell, containing one candidate cell ID and the CSI resource configuration for subsequent LTM. The gNB-CU may provide the LTM configuration ID mapping list to the candidate gNB-DU(s). The gNB-CU may request PRACH resources from the candidate gNB-DU(s). The gNB-CU may request the candidate gNB-DU(s) to provide the lower layer configuration for the purpose of generating the reference configuration or provide the lower layer part of the reference configuration to the candidate gNB-DU(s) (TS 38.401, Section 8.2.1.5, Step 3).

4. If the candidate gNB-DU accepts the request of LTM configuration, it responds with a UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message including the generated lower layer RRC configurations for the accepted target candidate cell (TS 38.401, Section 8.2.1.5, Step 4).

5. If the Early Sync Information Request IE is included in the UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message, the gNB-DU shall, if supported, include the Early Sync Information IE of the accepted candidate cell for early TA acquisition (early UL synchronisation), in the UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message (TS 38.473, Section 8.3.1.2).

6. If the LTM Configuration IE is included in the UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message, the gNB-CU shall, if supported, consider it as the generated configuration for LTM from the accepted candidate cell in the candidate gNB-DU (TS 38.473, Section 8.3.1.2).

Code_Instructions.description: Data structure defining the format of the F1AP UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message, used by the gNB-CU to initiate UE context establishment at the gNB-DU for LTM handover.
Data structure defining the format of the F1AP UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message, used by the gNB-DU to acknowledge successful UE context establishment to the gNB-CU.
Helper function used by the gNB-CU to generate the RRC part of the UE Context Setup Request message, including configurations for SRB, DRB, and other RRC parameters.
Helper function responsible for preparing or encoding the F1AP UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message, likely populating the F1AP message structure with information from higher layers.
Utility function to retrieve an existing UE context, which is essential for the gNB-CU to gather necessary information before initiating the F1AP UE Context Setup Request for handover.
Helper function to generate the RRC Security Mode Command message, which is part of the UE context setup and security configuration during handover.

Information_Elements.IE_Definition:
UEContextSetupRequestIEs F1AP-PROTOCOL-IES ::= {
    ...
    { ID id-LTMInformation-Setup                       CRITICALITY reject   TYPE LTMInformation-Setup                       PRESENCE optional },
    { ID id-LTMConfigurationIDMappingList             CRITICALITY reject   TYPE LTMConfigurationIDMappingList             PRESENCE optional },
    { ID id-EarlySyncInformationRequest               CRITICALITY reject   TYPE EarlySyncInformationRequest               PRESENCE optional },
    ...
}

UEContextSetupResponseIEs F1AP-PROTOCOL-IES ::= {
    ...
    { ID id-LTMConfiguration                          CRITICALITY reject   TYPE LTMConfiguration                          PRESENCE optional },
    { ID id-RequestedTargetCellID                     CRITICALITY reject   TYPE RequestedTargetCellID                     PRESENCE optional },
    { ID id-EarlySyncInformation                      CRITICALITY reject   TYPE EarlySyncInformation                      PRESENCE optional },
    ...
}
